Notes (Join the Discord for updates and:
(2016) Baby Keem releases "Come Thru" to soundcloud.

Type: This is the earliest period of Hykeem's creative work before adopting the Baby Keem name, when he released music independently under aliases like Hykeem Carter and HiiFii. Between 2016 and 2018, he primarily used SoundCloud to experiment with rapping and production. His first confirmed song, "Come Thru", during his freshman year of high school; it was removed from the Internet and he hopes "nobody will ever find it".

Notes (Join the Discord for updates and: Found on a live stream that's showing off Keem's old SoundCloud.

The first song keem made and is confirmed to be one of the few songs known on his old "hykeemc" Soundcloud account. When asked a interview with Complex about his early work he said "I was raised around a talented family—all my older uncles and cousins rapped so being around it so much, it came naturally. I made my first real song at 15, it was called "Come Thru. "Thankfully, nobody will be able to find it online, I hope". Was mentioned by one of his friends on Booman III | Ca$ino Documentary.

Notes (Join the Discord for updates and:
(2016) Youth is officially released
(May 5th, 2016) Reign officially releases.
Type: In 2016, Hykeem Carter released two early projects, Youth and Reign, marking the start of his music career before adopting the Baby Keem name. These releases reflect his initial experimentation with production and songwriting, laying the foundation for his later work. Notable tracks from this period include Rose Gold, which samples the Great Fairy Fountain theme from The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time, showcasing his early interest in blending atmospheric sounds with rap.

Notes (Join the Discord for updates and: “Rose Gold” is currently the earliest song to be recovered and was recorded by a then-15-year-old Keem. Released on Spotify on July 24, 2016. The track length is 3:45. Samples "Great Fairy Fountain" from "Zelda: Ocarina of Time". On December 10, 2023, a barely visible tracklist of Regin and Youth was found and revealed that Rose Gold was actually released twice with this version having a different vocal take.
Listed as on the tracklist in Genius but cannot be confirmed or verified as true with the currently available blurry tracklisting.

Notes (Join the Discord for updates and:
(May 5th, 2016) Reign officially releases.
(November 7th, 2016) Oct officially releases.
Type: In 2016, Hykeem Carter continued his early creative phase with the release of Oct on November 7, following his earlier projects Youth and Reign. The EP featured tracks like Options, originally released with a music video on December 7, 2016, before appearing on Oct. The song’s beat draws from the well-known sample used in “Antidote” and also incorporates “All I Need” by Lee Fields. According to RapTV, the 9-track EP showed traces of Carter's future musical style by "featuring a mix of bouncy, hard beats and slower, emotional cuts".

(November 7th, 2016) Oct Officially releases.
(January 6th, 2017) Black Nights officially releases.
Type: In early 2017, Hykeem Carter released Black Nights, following his previous projects Youth, Reign, and Oct. The project officially dropped on January 6, 2017, with its lead single Over That released a few days earlier on January 2. Black Nights featured tracks such as Way Back, Ride or Die (featuring Laura), Take A Flick, Chill, and Sorry, reflecting Hykeem’s continued experimentation with production and songwriting during this formative period. Some tracks, like Ride or Die, were recovered years later, while others remain partially unreleased or leaked, adding to the project’s elusive nature.

Type: In mid-2017, Hykeem Carter released PTNTL (Potential) on June 28, continuing his early independent work before adopting the Baby Keem name. The project remains largely lost, with only a few tracks confirmed, including FWM, Kissing Friends (released as the main single on June 6, 2017), and Flexin, which surfaced years later. Other songs such as Lil Chain, LeBron James, and a partially lost track known as Wake Up Like Tomorrow have been recovered or leaked over time.

Type: In early 2018, Hykeem Carter released Midnight on January 16, following his previous projects PTNTL and Black Nights. Although it showcased the same production as Oct, RapTV noticed that Carter's delivery was "more confident and loud". In an interview with Complex, he admitted that Midnight did not have any real artistic direction, and described the project as "just nine songs in my stash that I liked and put out".

Type: In mid-2018, Hykeem Carter released No Name on May 18, following his previous project Midnight. The EP featured tracks such as drunk, leverage (the only Hykeem Carter song to appear on Spotify/Apple Music), truce, 50 habits, and no smoke, continuing his self-produced style and experimental approach. This release marked one of the last projects under his real name before fully transitioning to the Baby Keem identity. The artwork was made by Anshor.

Type: After emailing a sample of his production to independent record label Top Dawg Entertainment, Hykeem contributed to the soundtrack album for the superhero film Black Panther (2018) as a producer. During the summer, he released two EPs, No Name and Hearts and Darts, and produced two songs on Jay Rock's third studio album Redemption. He considers Hearts and Darts, which was his first project under his stage name Baby Keem, to have personality that Midnight lacked.

Notes (Join the Discord for updates and:
(May 18th, 2018) Keem works with Cardo and Kendrick Lamar on his first mixtape under the name Baby Keem
(August 16th, 2018) Hearts & Darts released
(October 29th, 2018) The Sound of Bad Habit officially releases
Type: Following a name change from Hykeem Carter to HiiFii, and the release of his eighth EP “Hearts and Darts”, Keem would shift his focus to the release of his first commercial mixtape, dubbed The Sound of Bad Habit. CardoGotWings would be brought in as a key player for the production, and produce every song except for “A New Day” and “Extra”. Along with Cardo, Kendrick Lamar would be brought in to help with the recording process; his work mostly consisted of being another ear in the room and doing reference tracks for Keem. Despite the mixtape’s release on October 29, 2018, Kendrick Lamar remains uncredited as a writer on all streaming platforms.

Type: DIE FOR MY BITCH is Baby Keem’s second mixtape, following the release of his 2018 project, The Sound Of Bad Habit. This mixtape was released on July 19th, 2019, 8 months and 20 days after his first. The tape spawned three singles: “INVENTED IT”, “FRANCE FREESTYLE”, and Keem’s breakout hit “ORANGE SODA”. Kendrick Lamar also was brought to help with the recording process like with the song "BULLIES".

(September 22nd, 2021) The Melodic Blue receives it's "DLC"
Type: The Melodic Blue is Baby Keem’s debut studio album. The project has been in the works since late 2019 and was announced to be “on the way” via Instagram in early August 2020. On October 19, he released a hoodie, called “the idea hoodie,” with track titles from the album, such as “16,” “booman,” and “south africa” printed on it. The second single, “family ties” with Kendrick Lamar, was released on August 27. Following the latter single’s release, Keem began indicating that the album’s release was imminent. On September 6, Keem unveiled the LP’s artwork and release date in a tweet, alongside a trailer. On September 8, he revealed the tracklist.

(May 25, 2022) Keem hints at working on a new project
(August 5, 2023) "new album coming soon motherfuckers"
(January 2, 2024) Child With Wolves is revealed to be the name of the album
Type: child with wolves is Baby Keem’s sophmore studio album, following his 2021 debut, The Melodic Blue. Throughout 2022, Keem was on Kendrick Lamar’s The Big Steppers Tour, along with Tanna Leone. Towards the end of the tour, Keem and Scott Bridgeway played a few unreleased songs at a nightclub in Melbourne, Australia. After many rumours regarding the album’s title (some calling it “Classical Rage” or “Eerie Times,” after the usernames of two Instagram accounts that were found to be owned by Baby Keem), Baby Keem tweeted out the title on January 3, 2024.

Surprise-released on the pgLang YouTube channel on May 30th, 2023. Samples "PDLIF" by Bon Iver. Later, it was released for streaming on June 5, 2023. Unlike other Kendrick Lamar collaborations, this song was jointly released by Baby Keem and Kendrick Lamar. On Twitter, Keem confirmed the song uses the same flow as the Drake song "Sticky" off of his 2022 album. "Honestly, Nevermind" in a tweet saying, "Baby Keem & Kendrick Lamar - The Hillbillies (Sticky Dub)". Since the song's release, fans have speculated that the use of Drake's flow is used as a diss towards him.

Type: Ca$ino is Baby Keem’s long-awaited sophomore studio album. The album is Keem’s first album release in five (technically four) years, following his 2021 debut, The Melodic Blue. and its deluxe version in 2022. As Keem described the title at the album’s listening party. Listed as an seperate era to "Child With Wolves", this era will be updated when more information has surfaced to distinguish whether this is simply a name change or a completely different project.
